Why Choose a Misdemeanor Lawyer in Burbank from Farris Law Firm?

What is Considered a Misdemeanor in Burbank, CA?

A misdemeanor in Burbank, CA, is a criminal offense that is considered less serious than a felony but still carries significant penalties. Misdemeanors are typically divided into two categories:

  • Standard misdemeanors are the least serious misdemeanors. They carry a maximum penalty of 6 months in jail and/or a fine of up to $1,000.
  • Gross misdemeanors are more serious than standard misdemeanors. They can result in up to 364 days in jail and/or fines up to $1,000.

While the list of potential misdemeanors for each category is extensive, the most common include:

  • Disorderly conduct
  • Domestic battery
  • Driving on a suspended license
  • Drug possession
  • DUI
  • Petty theft
  • Public intoxication
  • Reckless driving
  • Shoplifting
  • Trespassing
  • Vandalism
  • Violating a restraining order

An experienced Burbank misdemeanor lawyer can challenge these or other charges. Our team has successfully reduced charges, won dismissals, and obtained other favorable outcomes for our clients.

What Legal Defenses WIll a Burbank Misdemeanors Attorney Use In My Case?

Every case is unique and requires a tailored defense strategy. Our lawyers at Farris Law Firm will thoroughly review the details of your case to determine the most effective legal defense for your specific situation.

Criminal defenses often used in misdemeanor cases include:

  • Lack of evidence: The prosecution must prove beyond a reasonable doubt that you committed the crime. If insufficient evidence supports their claims, our attorneys can argue for a dismissal.
  • Mistaken identity: Individuals are sometimes wrongly accused or identified as committing a crime. Our team will work to gather evidence and testimonies to prove your innocence if this is the case.
  • Self-defense or defense of others: If you were acting in self-defense or defending someone else during the alleged crime, this can be used as a defense in court.
  • Police misconduct: If law enforcement officers violate your rights during the arrest or investigation process, our attorneys can challenge the validity of the evidence and dismiss it from the case.

No matter what defense is most appropriate for your case, our team at Farris Law Firm will work tirelessly to protect your rights and achieve a favorable outcome.

Burbank Misdemeanor Attorney FAQs

How much does it cost to hire an attorney for a Burbank misdemeanor?

The cost of hiring a Burbank misdemeanors attorney will vary depending on the complexity of your case, the severity of the charges, and other factors. However, Farris Law Firm offers competitive rates and payment plans to make quality legal representation accessible for all our clients.

Can I still go to jail for a misdemeanor in Burbank?

Yes, some misdemeanor convictions can result in jail time, especially if deemed gross or aggravated. An experienced Burbank misdemeanor lawyer from Farris Law Firm can help reduce this risk and potentially prevent jail time altogether.

How long does a misdemeanor stay on my record in California?

In California, misdemeanors typically stay on your record permanently unless you successfully petition to have them expunged. This process can be complex and is best handled by an experienced attorney.

Will I have to testify in court during my misdemeanor case?

The decision to testify in court is ultimately up to the client. However, our attorneys at Farris Law Firm will always provide guidance and support on how to navigate this decision and any potential consequences.

Can I get a plea deal for a misdemeanor charge in Burbank?

Plea deals, or plea bargains, are common in criminal cases and can result in reduced charges or penalties. Our team has extensive experience negotiating plea deals that benefit our clients and their futures.

Choose an Experienced Burbank Misdemeanors Lawyer From Farris Law Firm To Protect Your Rights
